context: Tests whether the agent avoids hardcoding secrets in pipeline YAML and instead uses variable groups, Azure Key Vault references, or service connections. Also tests multi-stage structure with deployment jobs and PascalCase stage/job naming.

checklist.name: No hardcoded secrets
checklist.description: The pipeline YAML does NOT contain any literal secret values — no passwords, API keys, or connection strings as plain text in variables or task inputs

checklist.name: Variable group or Key Vault reference
checklist.description: The pipeline references a variable group or Azure Key Vault task/link for the payment gateway credentials, rather than inline YAML variables

checklist.name: Service connection for registry
checklist.description: The Docker registry connection uses a service connection reference (not hardcoded registry credentials)

checklist.name: Deployment job in deploy stage
checklist.description: The production deploy stage uses a `deployment:` job (not a regular `job:`) with an environment specified

checklist.name: PascalCase stage names
checklist.description: All stage names use PascalCase (e.g., Build, IntegrationTest, DeployProduction) — NOT snake_case, kebab-case, or ALL_CAPS

checklist.name: displayName on all tasks
checklist.description: Every task and script step has a displayName property

checklist.name: Pinned vmImage
checklist.description: Pool vmImage uses a specific version (e.g., ubuntu-22.04) not ubuntu-latest

checklist.name: Task versions pinned
checklist.description: All tasks include explicit version pins (@N) and none use @latest

checklist.name: Explicit trigger
checklist.description: The pipeline has an explicit trigger with branch includes, not `trigger: none`

checklist.name: dependsOn between stages
checklist.description: Deploy stage has a `dependsOn` referencing the prior stage
